description_long: This object is a vintage-style corded telephone with a cylindrical upright body and a conical base and top, reminiscent of early 20th-century candlestick phones. The handset is attached to the main body by a coiled copper-colored cord and features a flared mouthpiece and earpiece, both with a matte black finish. The base and top are finished in a marbled gray texture, while the central shaft is darker, possibly metal or painted wood. A brass horizontal bar supports the handset, and the rotary dial is prominently displayed on the front, encircled by metallic finger holes. The overall design is robust and utilitarian, with visible screws and metallic accents, suggesting durability and a classic aesthetic. This phone would likely be found in retro-themed urban environments, such as vintage shops, themed cafes, or as a decorative piece in public lobbies.
description: A vintage candlestick-style corded phone with a marbled gray base and top, black cylindrical shaft, brass accents, and a coiled cord connecting the handset. Features a prominent rotary dial and flared mouthpiece and earpiece.
description_view_0: Front view showing the upright body, rotary dial, and the handset hanging from the brass support with the coiled cord visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the flared mouthpiece and earpiece of the handset, as well as the marbled texture of the base and top.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the circular back of the earpiece and the full rotary dial, with the coiled cord draped to the side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view focusing on the handset's profile and the conical shapes of the base and top.
